bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid for myself. The opponent’s initial offer is valid but only gives me moderate utility, and with no messages or concession pattern yet, I should anchor on my strongest values across the high-weight issues.

I am targeting a very high utility bid (~0.961) because it is still extremely early at t=0.02 and the opponent has simply repeated the same low-utility offer to me (~0.442). I will make a small concession on the lowest-weight issueD toward their shown preference while holding firm on the three high-weight issues.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.88 rather than moving close to the opponent’s repeated offer, which is only about 0.44 for me. The opponent has held firm on the same valid bid three times with no messages, so I will make a modest concession by keeping issueD at their signaled valueC and softening issueC from my top valueB to valueA, while holding firm on the two highest-value issueA/issueB choices.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 rather than making major concessions. The opponent has repeated the same offer three times, worth about 0.44 to me, so there is no evidence of concession yet; I will only concede on low-weight issueD while holding my top values on the more important issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.08 and the opponent has repeated the same offer with no concession, worth only about 0.442 to me. Since their behavior is firm rather than cooperative, I will hold my most important issues at preferred values and only avoid repeating my immediately previous bid.

At t=0.10 it is still early, and the opponent has repeated the same bid worth only about 0.442 to me, so I am targeting a high-utility offer rather than making major concessions. To avoid simply repeating my last bid, I concede only on the lowest-weight issueD while holding my strongest preferences on the three high-weight issues.

At t=0.12 it is still early, and the opponent has repeated the same offer each time, which gives me only about 0.442 utility and shows no concession yet. I am therefore targeting a high-utility bid while avoiding an exact repeat of my last offer, keeping my strongest issues A and B at valueA and offering some flexibility on issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still early at t=0.14 and the opponent has repeated the same offer with no concession, worth only about 0.442 to me. I will keep my strongest positions on the high-weight issues A and B, while conceding only on low-weight issueD to the opponent’s repeated valueC and avoiding an exact repeat of my immediately previous bid.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.877, since we are still early at t=0.16 and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering the same bid worth only about 0.442 to me. With no messages and a fixed opponent pattern, I will hold firm on my highest-weight priorities issueA and issueB while making a modest concession on issueC and keeping the low-weight issueD at their repeated value.

At t=0.18 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for me rather than making major concessions; this bid gives me about 0.961 utility while conceding only on the low-weight issueD. The opponent has mostly repeated the same valid offer, worth only about 0.442 to me, and their latest move is even worse for me, so there is no evidence of meaningful concession yet.

At t=0.20 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession on the low-weight issueD. The opponent has held nearly firm around issueA=valueD, issueB=valueB, issueC=valueC, and recently changed issueD to valueD; since issueD matters least to me, I can accommodate that without giving up my key preferences on issues A, B, and C.

At t=0.22 it is still early, so I am targeting a strong utility bid while making limited concessions on lower-cost dimensions. The opponent has repeated essentially the same offer and recently worsened issueD for me, so I do not read meaningful concession from them; I will hold firm on high-value issueA and issueB while offering a new partial concession on issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.77 while making a meaningful concession from my earlier high-utility bids. The opponent has been very firm on issueA=valueD, issueB=valueB, issueC=valueC and recently issueD=valueD, so at t=0.24 I will hold my top values on the highly important issueA and issueB while conceding issueC and the low-weight issueD toward their pattern.

I am targeting a high utility bid (about 0.84) because it is still early at t=0.26 and the opponent has not meaningfully conceded; their repeated offer is worth only about 0.40–0.44 to me and recently worsened on issueD. I will concede the low-weight issueD to their recent valueD, while holding firm on the high-weight issueA and issueB and making only a moderate concession on issueC.

I am targeting a solid utility around 0.81 by holding firm on the two highest-value choices for issueA and issueB while conceding on issueC and the lower-weight issueD. At t=0.28 it is still early enough not to concede on issueA/issueB, and the opponent has been completely firm around issueA=valueD, issueB=valueB, issueC=valueC, so I am offering their repeated issueC=valueC signal without dropping my most important positions.

I am targeting a solid utility around 0.77 by holding firm on my highest-priority gains in issueA and issueB while conceding issueC and issueD toward values the opponent has repeatedly offered. At t=0.30 there is some time pressure to move beyond near-optimal bids, but the opponent has not meaningfully conceded and even shifted issueD to a worse value for me, so I should not give up the most valuable issues yet. There are no opponent messages to weigh, and all their offers have stayed within the valid domain.

I am targeting a high but not maximal utility bid around 0.81, since at t=0.32 there is still time and the opponent has not shown meaningful concession beyond repeating nearly the same offer. Their bids consistently emphasize issueA:valueD, issueB:valueB, and issueC:valueC, so I am conceding on issueC and using issueD:valueC, which they previously offered, while holding firm on my high-weight issueA and issueB preferences.

I am targeting a utility around 0.64 by conceding on issueB, issueC, and issueD while holding firm on issueA, one of my high-weight issues. At t=0.34 the opponent has repeated essentially the same valid bid and has not shown meaningful concession, so I am making a mid-game tradeoff rather than repeating my previous offer. Their bids consistently signal preference for issueB:valueB, issueC:valueC, and issueD:valueC/valueD, so this offer moves substantially toward that pattern while preserving value for me.

At t=0.36 I am still targeting a strong utility around 0.81 rather than dropping close to the opponent’s repeated low-utility offer. The opponent has mostly held firm on issueA:valueD, issueB:valueB, issueC:valueC and issueD:valueC/D, so I will concede on issueC and issueD while holding my highest-value positions on the more important issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a mid-game concession around utility 0.616, still well above the opponent’s repeated offer utility to me of about 0.442. The opponent has held nearly fixed on issueA=valueD, issueB=valueB, issueC=valueC, issueD=valueC, so I am testing a different tradeoff by conceding their apparent priority on issueA while holding my high-value issueB=valueA.

I am targeting a moderate utility of about 0.592 by conceding on issueA and issueB toward the opponent’s repeated pattern while holding firm on issueC, one of my high-weight issues. At t=0.40 there is time left, so I should not immediately collapse to the opponent’s full standing offer, but their lack of concession suggests a stronger compromise is needed. The opponent has consistently offered issueA=valueD, issueB=valueB, issueC=valueC, and issueD=valueC/D, with no natural-language messages.

I targeted a still-positive utility around 0.44 by matching the opponent’s repeatedly signaled bid, since they have shown almost no concession through t=0.42 and have returned to the same valid package many times. Time is not yet critical, but the long stalemate suggests that further insisting on my higher-utility variants is unlikely to move them soon. Their consistent offers indicate they strongly prefer issueA:valueD, issueB:valueB, issueC:valueC, and issueD:valueC, so I am conceding to that package to improve the chance of agreement.
